content.full_text:
TO:          CENTRAL FILE

THRU:        J. P. WITCHARD

FROM:        [illegible]

RE:          Code §§ and Regulations Referenced:
             8/7/10 (a)          8/20/25          1/8/15
             8/8/10 (b)          8/4/34
             8/1002.5(a)


June 3, 1976
                                                           Z-06-094

Mr. Richard Schmachel
California Labor Federation
995 Market Street, Suite 312
San Francisco, California  94103

Dear Mr. Schmachel:

     This letter is to confirm my advice to you by telephone on June 1,
1976.

     You stated the following facts and asked how the transactions
should be reported:

1.   The No on 15 Committee furnished materials for a mailing to
     the California Labor Federation's members, COPE paid the
     postmaster for the postage during the period covered by
     COPE's second pre-primary campaign statement. The Federation
     intends to reimburse COPE for the mailing costs sometime
     between now and August 10, 1976. I advised that COPE should
     report the postage expense on its second pre-primary campaign
     statement, and that the Federation should report making the
     reimbursement to COPE on the Federation's statement covering
     the period from January 7, 1975 through August 10, 1976 no
     later than August 17, 1976.

2.   By making a payment on behalf of another committee for which
     COPE will be reimbursed, COPE made a loan.  I advised that
     COPE should report the payment during the period made; and
     when COPE is reimbursed, it should report the reimbursement
     as a miscellaneous receipt.

3.   COPE has not contributed to any candidates in the primary
     election but has contributed to the campaign opposing
     Proposition 15. COPE sent a letter to members of its labor
     council recommending the election of certain candidates.
     COPE sent the letter independently of any of the candidates.
     I advised that since the endorsement letter does not constitute
     an in-kind contribution to the candidates, COPE should only
     file campaign statements with the Secretary of State, and the
     Registrars of Voters of San Francisco and Los Angeles counties.

Mr. Richard Schmachel                -2-                                June 3, 1976




4. On May 25, 1976 a newly-established committee pledged in
excess of $1,000 to another committee, and you asked whether
the new committee should report the pledge on Schedule D. I
responded that the recipient committee should report receiving
the pledge on Schedule D. The committee which made the pledge
should report it as an accrued expense on Schedule E and as an
expenditure on the summary portions of Schedules E and F when
actually paid.

Furthermore, if the pledge was received after the closing
date of the last campaign statement before the election, the
recipient must notify the filing officer with whom it files
its original campaign statements within 48 hours of receipt
of the pledge. For your information, I'm enclosing a fact
sheet which explains reporting of late contributions.

If we can be of further assistance, please contact our staff.

Sincerely,


NAN HAMBLETON
Special Compliance Representative


NH:mak
Enclosure

bcc: Jeanne-A √

embedding.qa_text: June 8, 1976 Mr. Richard Schmechel California Labor Federation 995 Market Street, Suite 312 San Francisco, California 94103 Dear Mr. Schmechel: This letter is to confirm my advice to you by telephone on June 1, 1976. You stated the following facts and asked how the transactions should be reported: 1. The No on 15 Committee furnished materials for a mailing to the California Labor Federation's members. COPE paid the postmaster for the postage during the period covered by COPE's second pre-primary campaign statement. The Federation intends to reimburse COPE for the mailing costs sometime between now and August 10, 1976. I advised that COPE should report the postage expense on its second pre-primary campaign statement, and that the Federation should report making the reimbursement to COPE on the Federation's statement covering the period from January 7, 1975 through August 10, 1976 no later than August 17, 1976. 2. By making a payment on behalf of another committee for which COPE will be reimbursed, COPE made a loan. I advised that COPE should report the payment during the period made; and when COPE is reimbursed, it should report the reimbursement as a miscellaneous receipt. 3. COPE has not contributed to any candidates in the primary election but has contributed to the campaign opposing Proposition 15. COPE sent a letter to members of its labor council recommending the election of certain candidates. COPE sent the letter independently of any of the candidates. I advised that since the endorsement letter does not constitute an in-kind contribution to the candidates, COPE should only file campaign statements with the Secretary of State, and the Registrars of Voters of San Francisco and Los Angeles counties. 4. On May 25, 1976 a newly-established committee pledged in excess of $1,000 to another committee, and you asked whether the new committee should report the pledge on Schedule D. I responded that the recipient committee should report receiving the pledge on Schedule D. The committee which made the pledge should report it as an accrued expense on Schedule F, and as an expenditure on the summary portions of Schedules E and F when actually paid. Furthermore, if the pledge was received after the closing date of the last campaign statement before the election, the recipient must notify the filing officer with whom it files its original campaign statements within 48 hours of receipt of the pledge. For your information, I'm enclosing a fact sheet which explains reporting of late contributions. If we can be of further assistance, please contact our staff. Sincerely, NAN HAMBLETON Special Compliance Representative NH:mak Enclosure bcc: Jeanne-A
